title: Fimbristylis pubisquama
taxonomy: Fimbristylis pubisquama is classified within the family Cyperaceae, which is a family of monocotyledonous plants commonly known as sedge and rushes. This family is a part of the order Poales, a group of flowering plants that includes grasses, sedges, and rushes. The order Poales is a part of the clade commelinids, which also includes the families Poaceae (grasses) and Bromeliaceae. The family Cyperaceae is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, and Fimbristylis pubisquama belongs to one of them. Overall, Fimbristylis pubisquama's classification reflects its close relationships with other plants in the Cyperaceae family and the larger clade of commelinids.

intro: Fimbristylis pubisquama, also known as the hairy-fruited fimbri, is a species of plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae). It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as treating infections. The bioactive constituents of Fimbristylis pubisquama include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
